assumptions.withdrawalOrderSummary: Sequential: cash, taxable, equity comp, traditional, Roth, HSA

statements: Educational illustration only - not tax, legal, financial, or medical advice. Figures are projections based on the assumptions below and will differ from actual results.

warnings: Some pre-65 years exceed 400% of the federal poverty line: no ACA credit (the cliff).

sources.figures: Seven brackets, 10%–37%. Standard deduction $16,100 single / $32,200 joint; +$2,050 / $1,650 each at 65+.
sources.label: Federal income tax brackets & standard deduction

sources.figures: $6,000 per person 65+, phasing out above $75,000 / $150,000 MAGI; temporary, expires after 2028.
sources.label: Senior bonus deduction (OBBBA)

sources.figures: $250,000 single / $500,000 joint of primary-residence gain excluded on sale (statutory since 1997, never indexed); depreciation after May 6, 1997 is not excludable.
sources.label: Home-sale gain exclusion (§121)

sources.figures: Provisional-income thresholds: up to 50% taxable above $25,000 / $32,000, up to 85% above $34,000 / $44,000 (fixed by statute, never indexed).
sources.label: Social Security benefit taxation

sources.figures: IRS Uniform Lifetime Table (Pub 590-B, 2022+); RMDs begin at age 73–75 per SECURE 2.0; QCD exclusion limit $111,000.
sources.label: Required minimum distributions & QCD

sources.figures: Non-qualified exclusion ratio from IRS Pub 939 Table V expected-return multiples (life expectancy by age); QLAC premium cap $210,000 excluded from RMD balances (SECURE 2.0).
sources.label: Annuity purchase (exclusion ratio & QLAC)

sources.figures: Principal-limit factors at a 5.875% expected rate: 35.1% of home value at 62 rising to 61.4% at 90 (planning default — a lender quote always wins); line/loan growth default 7.5%/yr (rate + 0.5% MIP).
sources.label: HECM reverse-mortgage principal limit
sources.publisher: HUD HECM PLF tables (as summarized for 2026)

sources.figures: Standard Part B $202.90/mo; five IRMAA tiers starting at $109,000 / $218,000 MAGI; Part D IRMAA surcharges $14.50-$91.00/mo.
sources.label: Medicare Part B & IRMAA

sources.figures: 2.8% COLA; taxable wage base $184,500; earnings-test exempt amounts $24,480 (pre-FRA) / $65,160 (FRA year); SSDI SGA $1,620/mo (non-blind); OASDI payroll tax 6.2% (employee).
sources.label: Social Security COLA & wage base

sources.figures: 2025 HHS guideline — $15,650 first person, +$5,500 each additional — applied to the 2026 ACA coverage year.
sources.label: Federal poverty guideline (ACA)

sources.figures: Applicable-percentage schedule per Rev. Proc. 2025-25 (2.10% under 133% FPL up to 9.96% at 300–400%), with the 400% FPL subsidy cliff restored (enhanced credits expired 12/31/2025).
sources.label: ACA premium tax credit

sources.figures: Par real yields as of 2026-06-30: 1.85% (5y), 2.05% (7y), 2.25% (10y), 2.55% (20y), 2.70% (30y). Prices TIPS-ladder quotes and the funded-ratio discounting; refreshed annually with the parameter packs.
sources.label: TIPS real-yield curve (income floor & bridge)

sources.figures: Brackets, standard deduction, Social Security treatment, and major retirement-income exclusions for all 50 states + DC, from each state revenue department.
sources.label: State income tax
